Research direction

Molecular insight for complex interfaces

My research uses molecular simulation, machine-learning force fields, and enhanced sampling to understand structure, dynamics, and transport at solid-liquid interfaces and in confined channels.

Solid-liquid interfaces

Graphene oxide, graphene, water, methanol, and membrane interfaces where local chemistry controls structure and reactivity.

ML force fields

Deep-learning potentials trained from quantum data to bridge electronic accuracy and molecular-dynamics timescales.

Nanofluidic transport

Water, ion, and proton transport through confined pores, including functionalized CNT porins and responsive gates.

Methods

Computational toolkit

Classical MD Ab Initio MD DFT MACE DeePMD Active Learning Metadynamics Umbrella Sampling Python Automation HPC Workflows
